subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: +1.2% | TSM blows past TP1 · NVDA at stop · ACHV PDUFA Friday

⚡ Exit Alerts — Action Needed Friday Open (June 20)

🚨 NVDA — STOP IMMINENT

Price $204.65 vs stop $204.00 — only $0.65 above stop (0.32%). One weak open and it's gone.

Stop validation:

Stop at $204 sits at the bottom of the confirmed near-term support zone ($200–$207). The 50-day MA is ~$208 — NVDA is below its 50-day MA. If $204 fails, next real support is the 200-day MA at ~$189. Stop is at a real level. TP1 $240 / TP2 $260 / TP3 $285 remain valid — but thesis requires holding above $204.

Decision for Friday open:

A — Let stop work. If NVDA opens below $204, exit triggers. Net loss if stopped: (1.3799 × $204) − $3 commission − $300.06 = −$21.56. Clean exit, thesis broken.

B — Preemptive exit Friday open. Same math. Useful if you want certainty rather than a weekend gap risk — NVDA news can drop over weekends.

Recommendation: Let the stop work. Don't anticipate it. Your call.

🔔 TSM — TP1 BREACHED. Take the profit?

TSM +6.94% on June 18 to $462.12. Your TP1 was $435 — the stock blew $27 through it. This is a new 52-week high (prior ceiling was ~$450). The +6.94% single-session move is institutional — likely a macro catalyst (AI foundry demand, US-Taiwan trade update, or TSMC revenue release; verify the reason).

Level validation:

Stop: $357 (below $388 breakout base — well defended) ✅
TP1: $435 — BREACHED (+6.1% above). Analyst avg target: $467–$473 (you're in the middle of the analyst range now).
TP2: $485 — 5.0% above current. Next real resistance / upper analyst target band.

🔄 TP1 Trim (original plan — sell 50%):

Sell: 0.112 shares at ~$462.12
Gross proceeds: $51.76 | Cost basis sold: 0.112 × $388.35 = $43.49
Gross P&L: +$8.27 | Exit commission: −$3.00 | Net P&L: +$5.27

Remaining: 0.112 shares at $462 = $51.76. Holds for TP2 ($485) / analyst target.

Counter-case: Analysts cluster at $467–$473. You're already in the middle of their bull range. Selling at TP1 now locks a real +19% win. Riding to TP2 ($485) means holding through potential mean-reversion from a 7% single-session move. Your call — both are reasonable.

⚠️ LLY — $15 from TP1. Monitor only.

At $1,098.78, LLY is $15 below TP1 ($1,114). Real resistance at $1,108 (confirmed prior high). TP1 trim plan: sell 30% (0.0296 shares) at $1,114.

Note: Gross P&L on 0.0296 sh = (1114−994.87)×0.0296 = +$3.52 | Commission: −$3.00 | Net: +$0.52 — barely clears minimum. If LLY stalls here, skip the trim and wait for TP2 ($1,243) on the remaining position. Don't pay $3 for $0.52 net.

✅ Entry at $34.26 vs $34.04 target — window is open. 18 days to PDUFA. IBKR approval is the only blocker. Director open-market conviction buy is the strongest signal in the file.

📡 Catalyst Plays — June 19, 2026

If approved: first new FDA-approved smoking cessation pharmacotherapy in ~20 years. Plant-derived alkaloid (nAChR partial agonist). Company holds an FDA Commissioner's National Priority Voucher for e-cigarette cessation — strong regulatory engagement signal.

Smart money: FDA CNPV voucher awarded — meaningful. No recent insider buys found. No unusual options data found. Pre-score: 3/5.

⚡ WATCH · 3/5

⚠️ TIMING WARNING: PDUFA is FRIDAY June 20 — tomorrow.

FDA decisions often drop premarket (6–9 AM ET = 1–4 PM Kuwait). If the announcement comes before US open (4:30 PM Kuwait), the stock will gap — by the time you can trade it the move is already priced in.

Pre-announcement window is effectively closed unless you place a limit order tonight/early Friday morning Kuwait time. Post-approval play (buying after confirmation) is a different, higher-risk trade. Say 'analyze ACHV' for deeper read. Your call.

Class II NDA resubmission after first CRL. FDA Type A meeting completed — constructive. OLC uses nanoparticle tech to reduce pill burden for dialysis patients. CEO publicly optimistic. Cash runway: $41.15M net into 2027. No dilution risk near-term.

10 days to PDUFA — time to properly research. Position sizing target: max 30% of portfolio ($251) → ~35 shares at $7.12. Say 'analyze UNCY' for deep dive first.

🔄 Slot 3 — Large-cap Tier A Fallback (no third catalyst play found)

ASMLASML Holding — EUV Lithography Monopoly SKIP — No event ≤45d

No confirmed binary event within 45 days. Shown for transparency — Tier A halal-compliant capital-parking candidate if cash needs a home after NVDA stop triggers. No action today.

💵 Cash Deployment — $207.81 deployable

Cash: $213.81 | Less $6 reserve | Less $3 buy commission = $204.81 into position

Option A — ACHV (PDUFA tomorrow, June 20):

57 shares at $3.57 = $203.49 position. Commission $3. Total: $206.49. Remaining cash after: $7.32 (at reserve floor). Position = 24.3% of portfolio — within 30% cap. ⚠️ TIMING: FDA could announce before US open tomorrow morning (1–4 PM Kuwait). If you want in pre-announcement, order must be in place tonight or very early Friday Kuwait time.

Option B — Add to existing:

NVDA risk score: HIGH (near stop — do NOT add). TSM past TP1 (overextended — do NOT add). LLY approaching resistance at TP1 — poor timing to enter. AVGO below entry ($407 vs $412) — possible, but small position size limits the upside.

Recommended: Hold cash as dry powder.

ACHV is almost certainly past the clean pre-announcement window. UNCY (June 29) has more runway for proper analysis. If NVDA stops out Friday, $278+ (net) is freed — that's the better trigger for deploying into UNCY or another setup. Hold until the NVDA decision clears. Your call.

🔄 Rotation — One move available

TSM TP1 trim frees $48.76 net — too small alone for a new position (below $75 minimum). But combined with cash: $213.81 + $48.76 = $262.57 total → $253.57 deployable (after $6 reserve + $3 buy commission).

If you trim TSM TP1 (sell 0.112 sh Monday open) AND want to deploy into UNCY (10 days to PDUFA): combined capital of $253 could enter ~35 shares at $7.12 = $249 position (30% of portfolio cap ✅). That's a clean rotation — book a TSM win, deploy into a near-term binary catalyst.

But: TSM is trading at analyst consensus now ($462 vs $467–$473 avg target). Riding to TP2 ($485) means ~5% more upside vs. locking in the TP1 gain. And UNCY carries meaningful second-CRL risk (45%). Do the UNCY analysis first, then decide. Your call.

📚 Day 23 of 30

Market-on-Close Auction (MOC)

The last 10 minutes of the US session (3:50–4:00 PM ET) runs as a formal closing auction. Institutions submit MOC (market-on-close) orders that don't touch the live tape — they queue up and print at the 4:00 PM close in a single matched-price event. Volume in this window is typically 5–10% of daily total; on end-of-quarter and index-rebalancing days it can hit 20%+. The 4:00 PM print from this auction is what every ETF, index fund, and institutional benchmark marks to — it's the only price that counts. TSM's +6.94% on June 18 closed strong in the auction — that kind of size at the bell usually means institutional demand locking in directional conviction before the holiday weekend.

Practical rule: never use a market order to exit in the last 10 minutes before 4:00 PM ET unless you accept auction-priced fills at whatever price clears. Use limit orders. End-of-quarter closes (June 30 is 11 days away) are the heaviest of the year — institutions window-dress portfolios, creating exaggerated close prints that often reverse in the opening session of the next quarter. Watch NVDA and TSM in the last 10 minutes of June 30 — the MOC auction that day will be unusually loud.

Also today: Index rebalancing — quarterly events where ETFs reconstitute holdings to match the index, generating massive predictable volume at month-end closes.

Prices as of June 18, 2026 close (last trading day before Juneteenth).
